instruction: Build a 3-level org chart for Meridian Labs on this board. LEVEL 1: CEO — Dana Whitfield (the seed node already on the board; keep it top-center). LEVEL 2 (four VPs, left to right): VP Engineering — Priya Natarajan; VP Product — Marcus Bell; VP Operations — Sofia Reyes; VP Marketing — Lena Kovacs. LEVEL 3: twelve teams, ALL on one flat bottom register: under Engineering → Platform Team, Frontend Team, Infra Team, Security Team; under Product → Design Team, Research Ops, Growth Team; under Operations → Finance Team, People Team; under Marketing → Brand Team, Events Team, Community Team. STYLE: leadership (CEO + all VPs) blue; all teams gray. Solid reporting lines CEO→each VP and each VP→its teams. ONE exception line: a dashed orange line from VP Marketing to Growth Team labeled 'acting'. Add a yellow sticky in the left margin: 'Interim: Growth also reports to Marketing while Product backfills the director role.' READABILITY (judge against our house reference standard): every VP centered over the midpoint of its own teams; CEO centered over the VP row; all twelve teams on one shared baseline; and the white gaps BETWEEN the four team clusters must look visually EVEN and generous — do not crowd the clusters together or let one gap balloon. Give every box, label, and the sticky clear air.

object.text: Interim: Growth also reports to Marketing while Product backfills the director role.

proposal.summary: Built the 3-level Meridian Labs org chart with centered leadership, a shared team baseline, even 128px inter-cluster gaps, reporting lines, the orange dashed “acting” cross-report, and interim sticky; retained W1 because Growth is Marketing’s exception—not its primary fan—and W2 because 32px intra-cluster vs 128px inter-cluster spacing is deliberate.
